controls.label: Does your business have a Verification Standards — Electronic process?
controls.remediation: Your organisation does not have a Verification Standards — Electronic process in place. You must establish a documented Verification Standards — Electronic process to meet your AML/CTF obligations. Review the controls below and implement the required procedures before completing this assessment.

controls.label: Does your AML/CTF program provide for the verification of KYC information collected about customers by means of reliable and independent electronic data?
controls.detail-label: Describe the electronic verification methods and data sources used in your program
controls.remediation: Your Verification Standards — Electronic process must establish a framework for using reliable and independent electronic data to verify KYC information. Update your AML/CTF program to define which electronic verification methods and data sources you will employ, as required under rule 4.10.1.

controls.label: Have you implemented risk-based systems and controls to assess whether electronic data used for verification is reliable and independent?
controls.detail-label: Explain your risk-based assessment methodology for electronic data reliability
controls.remediation: Your Verification Standards — Electronic process must include risk-based systems and controls to determine whether electronic data is reliable and independent. Implement a documented assessment methodology that evaluates the factors specified in rule 4.10.2(1), including data accuracy, security, currency, comprehensiveness, source verification, government/legislative basis, and authentication capability.

controls.label: Have you pre-defined which reliable and independent electronic data sources your reporting entity will use for verification of KYC information?
controls.detail-label: List the approved electronic data sources and explain why each is considered reliable and independent
controls.remediation: Your Verification Standards — Electronic process must specify which reliable and independent electronic data sources your reporting entity will use for verification. Document the approved electronic data sources and provide justification for each selection based on your risk-based assessment, as required under rule 4.10.2(2).

controls.label: Have you established pre-defined tolerance levels for matches and errors when verifying KYC information against electronic data?
controls.detail-label: Describe your pre-defined tolerance levels for matches and errors, and explain how these levels reflect your risk-based approach
controls.remediation: Your Verification Standards — Electronic process must include pre-defined tolerance levels for matches and errors to guide verification decisions. Document your tolerance thresholds (e.g. exact match required, partial match acceptable) and explain how they align with your risk assessment, as required under rule 4.10.2(3).

controls.label: Does your process include provisions to confirm KYC information by independently initiating contact with the person the customer claims to be?
controls.detail-label: Describe the circumstances under which you initiate independent contact and how you document this confirmation
controls.remediation: Your Verification Standards — Electronic process must specify whether and how you will confirm KYC information by independently initiating contact with the person the customer claims to be. Define the risk-based triggers for independent contact, the contact methods used, and your documentation requirements, as required under rule 4.10.2(4).

groups.title: Process Confirmation
groups.description: Before answering the detailed compliance questions, confirm whether your organisation operates this business process.

groups.title: Electronic Verification Framework
groups.description: Establishes the overall framework for using reliable and independent electronic data to verify KYC information collected about customers.

groups.title: Data Reliability Assessment
groups.description: Risk-based systems and controls to evaluate whether electronic data is reliable and independent, considering accuracy, security, currency, comprehensiveness, source verification, government/legislative basis, and authentication capability.

groups.title: Electronic Data Selection
groups.description: Pre-definition of the reliable and independent electronic data sources that your reporting entity will use for verification purposes.

groups.title: Match and Error Tolerance Standards
groups.description: Pre-defined tolerance levels for matches and errors that guide verification decisions and reflect the reporting entity's risk-based approach.

groups.title: Independent Contact Confirmation
groups.description: Procedures for confirming KYC information by independently initiating contact with the person the customer claims to be, where appropriate.
